children.value: Sometimes the Portal must be taken down for updates. For instance, the EC2 the Portal runs on needs to be respawned for updates.
To help facilitate communication with users, an Under Construction page can be enabled. All traffic to the Portal will be redirected to this page.
To enable the page, log into the AWS account and go to EC2 console.
Go to the Portal load balancer, select the
In the dropdown Actions menu, select Edit Rule.
Set the instance target group weight to
. Set the lambda target group weight to
At the bottom of the page Save Changes.
Changes should take affect almost immediately.
To revert changes after updating, repeat the above steps except change the target group weights so that the instance gets
The following documentation is older and must be used with caution.

These secrets will be used to communicate with SES to send emails. “SMTP credentials consist of a username and a password. When you click the Create button below, SMTP credentials will be generated for you.” The credentials are AWS access keys, like as used in local aws configs. They are valid for the whole region.
Create a verified email and take out of sandbox.
. The IAM User Name should be unique and easy to find within IAM. On user creation, SMTP credentials will be created.
Add username and password as given previously in the following format:
AWS Secrets Manager: Create SSO token

children.label: AWS Secrets Manager: Create SSO token

children.value: This token will be used by the labs to communicate and authenticate with the portal. All labs and the portal share this token. It is imperative that this remains secret. The form of the token is very specific. Use the following process to create the token.

from cryptography.fernet import Fernet

api_token = Fernet.generate_key()
api_token
For local dev development, one can use a local docker registry.
Otherwise, the remote docker images will be stored in AWS ECR, as setup by CloudFormation
If production, upload the Cloudformation template
Once the cloudformation is done, go to EC2 Connect of the portal EC2, log onto the server and

.
Note that you will be warned about reformatting the DB volume. If this is the first time running (as it should be), do so.

If locally, go to the root of the docker repo.
Then setup prerequisites via

. The name of the config doesn’t matter (except it cannot be labs.run.yaml)
Update labs.{maturity}.yaml as needed
If production, clear out the registry images, delete the CloudFormation setup, delete db ebs snapshots, and delete logs.
and then stop the localhost registry (if being used).
In production, normally the logs will show up in CloudWatch.
If the Portal DB needs to be replaced by a snapshot backup, do the following.
Unless otherwise sated, all of these steps take place within EC2 Connect.
Elevated permissions will be needed via
This procedure assumes that the usual DB volume is present and being used. We only want to update the DB file.
, it is assumed that the AZ of the EC2’s subnet is set as us-west-2a.
a. From the EC2 console, select the snapshot that will be restored. Get the SNAPSHOT_ID, e.g. snap-0c0dbee2e7c9f0c12
b. From the EC2 console, select the portal EC2. Get the EC2_INSTANCE_ID, e.g. i-0ca96843e97d9bd29
c. First run a dry run to make sure permissions are available for EBS volume creation.

Then actually create an EBS volume from the backup snapshot.
 ```
 aws ec2 create-volume \
     --availability-zone us-west-2a \
     --snapshot-id $SNAPSHOT_ID \
     --tag-specifications 'ResourceType=volume,Tags=[{Key=Name,Value=portal-db-backup}]'
 ```

 From response output, get VOLUME_ID, e.g. vol-0a0869b5ab9f77090
First run a dry run to make sure permissions are available.
If you get an error message something like
/wrong fs type, bad option, bad superblock
then you cannot mount the filesystem. AWS’s way of handling volumes makes things difficult.
Since we are working with a temporay mount, run the following instead:
Create a backup of the old DB file within the filesystem for just in case
Unmount and detach backup volume from EC2
